name: Ascent to the Mirador del Valle — circular via the Camino Viejo to the Santuario
name_original: Subida al Mirador del Valle — circular por el Camino Viejo al Santuario
region_intro: Jogging around Andújar offers diverse routes through the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, characterized by rolling hills and Mediterranean forests. The region features varied terrain, from gentle riverside paths along the Jándula and Yeguas rivers to more challenging trails in the northern mountainous areas. Elevations range from 400 to 1,290 meters, providing options for different fitness levels. This natural landscape, rich in holm oaks and cork oaks, makes for varied and engaging running experiences. Best jogging routes around Andújar The most popular jogging route is Mota de la Isla by the Río Guadalquivir — circular from Andújar, a 3.1 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 35 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Guadalquivir river, offering flat terrain suitable for a relaxed run. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra Morena Trail: Santuario de la Cabeza Loop, a difficult 11.4 miles (18.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and panoramic views within the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park. Local runners also love the La Ropera and Rincón del Jándula Loop, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forests and along the Jándula river, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Jogging around Andújar is defined by Mediterranean forests, rolling hills, and scenic river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to more challenging trails with significant ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Andújar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Andújar?
A: Komoot offers a wide selection of over 50 running routes around Andújar. These routes vary in difficulty and length, providing options for all fitness levels, from easy riverside jogs to challenging trail runs through the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Andújar?
A: The jogging trails in Andújar offer diverse terrain, primarily within the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park. You'll find rolling hills, Mediterranean forests with holm and cork oaks, and paths along scenic rivers like the Jándula and Yeguas. Some routes feature gentle, flat sections, while others, particularly in the northern mountainous areas, present more challenging ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, there are several easy running routes. A great option for a relaxed jog is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305183516/mota-de-la-isla-by-the-rio-guadalquivir-circular-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota de la Isla by the Río Guadalquivir — circular from Andújar&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.1-mile (4.9 km) route follows the Guadalquivir river, offering flat terrain perfect for an easy run.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running routes with significant elevation in the region?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1301695292/sierra-morena-trail-santuario-de-la-cabeza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail: Santuario de la Cabeza Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and panoramic views within the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989787073/santuario-virgen-de-la-cabeza-circular-trail-running-in-the-sierra-de-andujar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza - circular trail running in the Sierra de Andujar natural park&lt;/a&gt;, which also features substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Andújar are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305183516/mota-de-la-isla-by-the-rio-guadalquivir-circular-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota de la Isla by the Río Guadalquivir — circular from Andújar&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1301695292/sierra-morena-trail-santuario-de-la-cabeza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Morena Trail: Santuario de la Cabeza Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: The running routes often pass through the stunning Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, offering views of Mediterranean forests, rolling hills, and rivers. You might encounter significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1279995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cabeza&lt;/a&gt;, or scenic spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4342897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2862464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinarejo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque backdrops for your run.

Q: Are there any running trails that follow rivers or offer waterside views?
A: Yes, several routes in Andújar feature beautiful waterside views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305183516/mota-de-la-isla-by-the-rio-guadalquivir-circular-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota de la Isla by the Río Guadalquivir — circular from Andújar&lt;/a&gt; follows the Guadalquivir river. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1301716481/la-ropera-and-rincon-del-jandula-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ropera and Rincón del Jándula Loop&lt;/a&gt; leads through Mediterranean forests and along the Jándula river, offering serene waterside running experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Andújar?
A: Andújar's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for jogging, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer mornings and evenings can also be pleasant, but midday runs might be too hot. Winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, perfect for longer runs, though some higher elevation trails might be chillier.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Andújar?
A: For families looking to jog together, easier and shorter routes are recommended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305183516/mota-de-la-isla-by-the-rio-guadalquivir-circular-from-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota de la Isla by the Río Guadalquivir — circular from Andújar&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice due to its flat terrain and manageable distance, suitable for various fitness levels within a family.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park?
A: The Sierra de Andújar Natural Park is generally dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially endangered species like the Iberian lynx. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect any specific signage or regulations on individual trails.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a good mix of scenery and a moderate challenge?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1301716481/la-ropera-and-rincon-del-jandula-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ropera and Rincón del Jándula Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic option. This 7.8-mile (12.5 km) route takes you through beautiful Mediterranean forests and along the Jándula river, offering a moderate challenge with varied scenery that can be completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while running in the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park?
A: The Sierra de Andújar Natural Park is a vibrant Mediterranean ecosystem. While jogging, you might spot various bird species, and if you're lucky and discreet, you could catch a glimpse of significant wildlife. The park is home to endangered species such as the Iberian lynx, black vulture, and imperial eagle, adding to the immersive natural experience.
tips: Old rural road that leads to the Ermita de San Ginés which is located at the top of the mountains above the town of Andújar.
It is a place to stop and look at the achievements made in height, because from there to Andújar we have reached a few meters of difference in altitude.
